intro: Thymus carstiensis, also known as Carstea's thyme, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, characterized by its small, white to pinkish flowers and tiny, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Bioactive constituents of Thymus carstiensis include essential oils containing thymol and carvacrol, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Thymus carstiensis is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infused oils, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
